title: Three review questions
purpose: Open with the usable product promise: for each real stacked commit, show its Git identity, GraphReFly-generated graph delta, and exact parent code diff together.

title: Point at a compatible repository
purpose: Create and review a small non-flagship repository with four real commits through the public review --repo --base --head command, while stating the intentionally narrow v1 compatibility contract.

title: Blueprint comes from GraphReFly
purpose: Select the derive commit and show that the target repository emits GraphBlueprint v2 while public upstream parse, hash, Mermaid, and delta helpers produce the readable diagram and structural events.

title: One selection, synchronized evidence
purpose: Select the mount commit and show its immutable OID, upstream Blueprint, subgraph and node events, and GitHub-style split parent diff change together.

title: Graph delta explains code delta
purpose: Select the metadata-only commit and show node-changed audit::status beside the exact one-line source change, proving the diagram is revision evidence rather than a hand-authored fixture.

title: Narrow, honest trust boundary
purpose: Explain fail-closed detached execution, exact dependency continuity, linear range and cost bounds, collapsible provenance, and the neutral semantic-not-configured state rather than inventing a gate verdict.

title: Codex, GPT-5.6, and the roadmap
purpose: Distinguish the deterministic generic review product from the optional GPT-5.6 planning layer built with Codex, then record DAG, hosted SaaS, planning, mutual exclusion, edit scopes, conflict avoidance, and revert as explicit roadmap work.
